title: Compassion Benchmark Daily Briefing — July 4, 2026
headline: Iran's Khamenei funeral opens July 4 — the first Supreme Leader transition in 35 years; Russia strikes Kyiv again; El Salvador's band-crossing proposal carries forward at 15.0 of 100, unpublished.
summary: July 4, 2026 is a confirmation-dominant cycle: all 15 assessed entities hold at their published scores. Zero new proposals were generated. The El Salvador downgrade proposal — proposing 15.0 of 100 in the Critical band against a published score of 20.3 of 100 — carries forward as pending for a third day. The published El Salvador score remains 20.3 of 100. Iran's Khamenei state funeral opens today, beginning the Islamic Republic's first Supreme Leader transition in 35 years. Iran holds at 2.5 of 100. The funeral is a state ceremony; Iran's score turns on new harm, not political transition. US-Iran nuclear talks are paused to July 11. Mojtaba Khamenei is the leading succession candidate. Russia's July 4 Kyiv strike and the July 2-3 residential strikes together have killed over 30 civilians in three days — the record July toll continues. Russia holds at 0 of 100; Ukraine holds at 50.0 of 100. Sudan's Human Rights Watch El Obeid imminent-atrocities warning (July 3) documents 500,000 people at siege risk and 105,000 additional internally displaced persons; Sudan holds at 0 of 100 as the RSF is a non-state actor. Pakistan entered the priority cluster for the first time on June 28-29 cross-border strike evidence; Pakistan is confirmed at 17.2 of 100. Fifteen false-positive screens were documented — tying the series record set July 3. Four rotation-state integrity corrections were applied this cycle: China rank, Ukraine rank, Russia rank, and Pakistan composite and rank — all corrected to published index values.

pipeline.elSalvadorHandling: Pending 2026-07-02 downgrade proposal (20.3 to 15.0, Developing to Critical) confirmed to stand. Today's evidence is the same already-captured basis; no second proposal emitted. Published score remains 20.3. Pending proposal file unchanged. Carry-forward day 3.

topSignals.title: Iran 2.5 of 100: Khamenei Funeral Opens July 4 — First Supreme Leader Transition in 35 Years; Mojtaba as Leading Successor; US-Iran Talks Paused to July 11; Protest Watch Active
topSignals.whyItMatters: Iran's Khamenei state funeral opened today — the Islamic Republic's first Supreme Leader transition since 1989. Iran holds at 2.5 of 100. The funeral is a state ceremony; the score turns on new harm, not political transition. A documented crackdown during the funeral window is the forward trigger.
topSignals.description: Iran holds at 2.5 of 100 — the near-floor Critical band. The July 4-9 Khamenei state funeral is the Islamic Republic's first Supreme Leader transition in 35 years. Khamenei's February 28 assassination was Israel's conduct, scored against Israel. The July 4 funeral itself is a state ceremony — procedural, not a harm event. Mojtaba Khamenei is the leading succession candidate. The Assembly of Experts is expected to convene during the funeral window to confirm a successor. US-Iran nuclear talks in Doha — which had been underway — are paused until at least July 11, a specific new date created by the political transition. Iran's score of 2.5 of 100 reflects its own prior conduct: 784-plus executions year-to-date (a 37-year high), the Strait of Hormuz central channel mine deployment, and ongoing Gulf strikes already priced into the existing score. No new Iranian own-conduct harm in the July 4 window was identified. The protest-suppression watch flag is open for the full funeral window: a documented lethal crackdown on mourners or demonstrators, or mass arrest of political figures, during July 4-9 would constitute a new scored harm event. Confirmed at 2.5 of 100; confidence: high.

evidence.claim: Reuters July 4, 2026: Khamenei state funeral begins July 4; Mojtaba Khamenei identified as leading succession candidate; Assembly of Experts expected to convene during funeral window; US-Iran Doha nuclear talks paused to July 11; 784-plus executions documented year-to-date — the highest annual rate in 37 years.

topSignals.title: Russia 0 of 100: July 4 Kyiv Strike Extends Record July Toll — 30-Plus Killed in July 2-4 Window; EU Special Tribunal Active; Ukraine 50.0 of 100 Confirmed
topSignals.whyItMatters: Russia's July 4 Independence Day strike on Kyiv continues a campaign that has killed over 30 civilians in three days — the fastest July casualty rate on record. Russia holds at 0 of 100 — the absolute floor. Ukraine holds at 50.0 of 100 on its civilian-protection response.
topSignals.description: Russia holds at 0 of 100 — the absolute floor, reinforced for the thirteenth consecutive cycle. The July 4 strike on Kyiv follows the July 2-3 residential strikes that killed 30-plus civilians in the three-day window. Russia's July 2026 campaign is tracking above the record pace set in the prior cycle. The EU Special Tribunal for crimes in Ukraine remains active; 20,500-plus children have been documented as forcibly deported. Ukraine holds at 50.0 of 100 in the Functional band. Ukraine's civilian-protection response — coordinated metro-sheltering and evacuation during the strike campaign — is consistent with the Functional band. Under the benchmark's attribution rule, harm inflicted by one state on another is scored against the source state, not the target. Russia's deliberate targeting is Russia's conduct. Ukraine's defense is Ukraine's conduct. Both scores reflect that separation. Russia cannot go lower than 0 of 100. Ukraine's forward trigger — documented state failure to protect its own civilians, or discriminatory relief — has not occurred. Three rotation-state rank drifts were corrected this cycle: China rank (154 to 148), Ukraine rank (48 to 53), and Russia rank (180 to 188) — all corrected to published index values.

evidence.claim: OHCHR Ukraine Monitoring Mission July 2026: total documented civilian casualties reach 62,716 as of July 1, 2026; the 2026 casualty rate exceeds the 2025 rate; continued deliberate targeting of residential areas, city centers, and energy infrastructure attributed to Russian forces.

Ukraine State Emergency Service July 4, 2026: Russia struck Kyiv on July 4; the July 2-4 window has produced over 30 civilian deaths; coordinated metro-sheltering of residents continues; EU Special Tribunal for crimes in Ukraine remains active.

topSignals.title: Sudan 0 of 100: HRW Issues El Obeid Imminent-Atrocities Warning — 500,000 at Siege Risk; 105,000 Additional IDPs; Amnesty El Fasher CAH Report Stands; Floor Reinforced
topSignals.whyItMatters: Human Rights Watch's July 3 El Obeid warning documents 500,000 people at imminent siege risk in North Kordofan — the RSF's latest offensive. Sudan holds at 0 of 100. The RSF is a non-state actor; this conduct is non-state harm. The HRW 'imminent' framing raises forward-catastrophe probability.
topSignals.description: Sudan holds at 0 of 100 — the absolute floor. The July 4 assessment window carries forward two major evidence packages from the prior cycle. First: Human Rights Watch's July 3 El Obeid imminent-atrocities warning. El Obeid is the capital of North Kordofan state. The RSF offensive threatens 500,000 people in El Obeid and 105,000 additional internally displaced persons in surrounding zones. The 'imminent' framing from a Tier-3 source is analytically significant — it raises the forward probability of a fast-moving humanitarian catastrophe that could develop within the assessment window. Second: Amnesty International's July 2026 El Fasher crimes-against-humanity report — documenting RSF mass civilian killings, systematic rape, and forced displacement — stands from the July 3 cycle. Combined, these two packages represent the highest-severity evidence accumulation in Sudan's July series. The RSF is a non-state armed group. Under the benchmark's attribution rule, non-state actor harm is not scored against the state. Sudan at 0 of 100 reflects the Sudanese government's own prior conduct: the breakdown of state protection and al-Burhan's documented rejection of ceasefire proposals. The scored upgrade trigger — an implemented civilian-protecting ceasefire with unobstructed humanitarian access — has not occurred.

evidence.claim: Human Rights Watch July 3, 2026: El Obeid imminent-atrocities warning — RSF offensive in North Kordofan threatens 500,000 people in El Obeid and 105,000 additional internally displaced persons in surrounding zones; UNHCR humanitarian truck destroyed; imminent-siege risk documented.

Amnesty International July 2026: RSF forces committed crimes against humanity in El Fasher, North Darfur — including mass civilian killings, systematic rape, and forced displacement; El Fasher is the last major city in North Darfur not under RSF control.

topSignals.title: El Salvador 20.3 of 100 Published — Band-Crossing Proposal at 15.0 of 100 Carries Forward Day 3; Pakistan Enters Priority Cluster; Venezuela Day 10; China Article 63 Day 4
topSignals.whyItMatters: El Salvador's proposed Critical-band score of 15.0 of 100 carries forward for a third day — the published score stays at 20.3 of 100. Pakistan enters the priority cluster for the first time on cross-border strike evidence, confirmed at 17.2 of 100. Venezuela's 14-day checkpoint is four days out.
topSignals.description: El Salvador holds a published score of 20.3 of 100 in the Developing band. The July 2, 2026 proposal to move to 15.0 of 100 — a Developing-to-Critical band crossing, delta -5.3 — carries forward as pending on July 4, the third consecutive carry-forward cycle. No new evidence was identified on July 4 that was not already captured in the July 2 proposal. The July 4 assessment encountered the same evidence basis (indefinite re-election constitutional amendment, Cristosal exile, 86 political prisoners, child life-sentences) and correctly applied the duplicate-control rule: no second proposal emitted. The published score of 20.3 of 100 remains operative. Pakistan enters the priority cluster for the first time. The June 28-29 cross-border strikes on TTP positions in Afghanistan generated a 36-civilian-death claim from Afghan Taliban sources — disputed by Pakistan. The harm was inflicted on Afghan territory. The disputed-attribution rule was applied: Pakistan is confirmed at 17.2 of 100. Pakistan's rotation-state composite had drifted to 15.3; corrected to 17.2 (published). Venezuela holds at 18.0 of 100 on Day 10 of the June 24 earthquake response. The official death toll stands at 2,645. A single-source pathologist undercount claim remains sub-threshold. The July 8 checkpoint — the 14-day standard mark for distinguishing capacity failure from deliberate obstruction — is four days out. China holds at 19.5 of 100. The Ethnic Unity and Progress Law's Article 63 diaspora liability has been in force for four days without a documented enforcement action.

evidence.quote: We are forced to choose between prison or exile
evidence.claim: NPR July 2025: Cristosal — El Salvador's leading human rights organization after 25 years — forced out of the country under threat of prosecution. Part of the July 2, 2026 pending proposal basis; not new on July 4.

evidence.quote: El Salvador's Legislative Assembly approved a constitutional amendment allowing indefinite presidential re-election with 57 votes in favor and three opposed
evidence.claim: Al Jazeera August 2025: indefinite re-election codified; term extended to 6 years; presidential runoff removed. Part of the July 2 pending proposal basis.

topSignals.title: DRC 2.3 of 100: Ebola July Peak Window Active; 15 False-Positive Screens Tie Series Record; Four Rotation-State Integrity Corrections Applied
topSignals.whyItMatters: DRC's Ebola outbreak continues through its July natural peak with multi-continent spread confirmed. Fifteen false-positive screens tie the series record set yesterday — validating framework stability. Four rotation-state drift corrections were applied across China, Ukraine, Russia, and Pakistan.
topSignals.description: The Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) holds at 2.3 of 100. The Bundibugyo-strain Ebola outbreak continues through its July natural seasonal peak. The multi-continent spread — Uganda, France, and Germany — confirmed in the prior cycle stands. Total cases: 1,406 confirmed; 438 deaths. No approved Bundibugyo vaccine. Contact tracing continues at approximately 45 percent. The floor-crossing trigger — documented deliberate government obstruction of WHO response corridors in Haut-Uele, Ituri, or North Kivu — has not occurred. DRC government conduct has not included documented WHO-corridor obstruction in any cycle. M23-related harm in eastern DRC is attributed to Rwanda-backed forces, not DRC government. Fifteen false-positive screens tie the series record set July 3. The July 4 cluster added Pakistan cross-border disputed attribution as a new simultaneous false-positive category. All seven false-positive category types are active simultaneously — matching the July 3 all-categories milestone. All 15 screens correctly disposed of under the established framework without requiring rule modifications. Four rotation-state integrity corrections were applied: China rank (154 to 148), Ukraine rank (48 to 53), Russia rank (180 to 188), and Pakistan composite (15.3 to 17.2) and rank (147 to 154). The Pakistan composite correction of 2.0 points is the largest single-entity composite correction in the July series. Confirmed at 2.3 of 100; confidence: high.

evidence.claim: WHO July 2026: Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak at 1,406 confirmed cases and 438 deaths; multi-continent spread confirmed (Uganda, France, Germany); PHEIC active; July natural peak window active; no approved Bundibugyo vaccine; contact tracing at approximately 45 percent.

boundaryWatch.trigger: The July 2, 2026 proposal to move El Salvador to 15.0 of 100 in the Critical band carries forward as pending on July 4 — the third carry-forward cycle. The 2027 election under the indefinite re-election amendment, further mass-detention escalation, or additional human-rights-defender imprisonment would add scored downward pressure at the proposed Critical level. Due-process restoration and civil-society reinstatement are the upgrade triggers.
boundaryWatch.note: El Salvador at 20.3 of 100 sits 0.3 points above the Developing-to-Critical boundary at 20.0. The July 2 assessment proposed 15.0 of 100 — a band crossing with a delta of -5.3. The proposal carries forward as pending on July 4. Published score remains 20.3. Third carry-forward cycle; no new evidence identified July 4.

boundaryWatch.trigger: First documented Article 63 enforcement action under the Ethnic Unity and Progress Law — prosecution, forced rendition, or family detention of a diaspora member — is the next scored threshold event. Law has been in force since July 1; no enforcement documented through July 4 (Day 4).
boundaryWatch.note: China at 19.5 of 100 Critical sits 0.5 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary at 20.0. Ethnic Unity Law in force since July 1; Article 63 diaspora liability active through July 4 without documented enforcement. Fourth boundary-proximity watch cycle. Rotation-state rank corrected from 154 to published 148.

boundaryWatch.trigger: EU AI Act full applicability on August 2 — 29 days away. White House 30-day vetting framework resolution is the next forward factor.
boundaryWatch.note: Anthropic at 59.1 of 100 Functional sits 0.9 points below the Functional-to-Established boundary at 60.0. Fourteenth consecutive cycle in this range. EU AI Act applies August 2 — 29 days away. Not assessed tonight.

boundaryWatch.trigger: NLRB review of the unfair-labor-practice charge filed after Apple closed its Towson, Maryland store — its first unionized store. An NLRB complaint or ruling is the conversion trigger.
boundaryWatch.note: Apple at 59.4 of 100 Functional sits 0.6 points below the Functional-to-Established boundary at 60.0. The Congressional Labor Caucus call for an NLRB investigation remains pre-adjudication. Tenth boundary-watch cycle. Not assessed tonight.

boundaryWatch.trigger: A new ceasefire agreement AND Lebanese Armed Forces assuming sovereign control of southern Lebanese territory AND reconstruction delivery together constitute scored positive own-conduct — the upgrade trigger from 17.2 toward 20.0.
boundaryWatch.note: Lebanon at 17.2 of 100 Critical sits 2.8 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary at 20.0. Israeli strikes continue; no new ceasefire framework as of July 4. Harm from Israeli strikes is attributed to Israel, not Lebanon. Fourth boundary-proximity watch cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Independently verified deliberate civilian targeting or a disproportionate mass-casualty cross-border strike is the downgrade trigger. Durable equitable relief scale-up with Afghan-deportation reversal is the upgrade trigger toward 20.0.
boundaryWatch.note: Pakistan at 17.2 of 100 Critical sits 2.8 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary at 20.0. Assessed tonight as a new priority cluster entrant. June 28-29 cross-border TTP strikes; disputed attribution. Rotation-state composite corrected from 15.3 to published 17.2; rank corrected from 147 to 154. Third boundary-proximity watch cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: July 8 checkpoint — documented aid obstruction or deliberate diversion is the downgrade trigger; sustained effective transparent relief is the upgrade trigger toward 20.0. Action and Accountability watch flags open.
boundaryWatch.note: Venezuela at 18.0 of 100 Critical sits 2.0 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary at 20.0. Day 10 earthquake response; official death toll 2,645; July 8 checkpoint four days out. Single-source undercount allegation sub-threshold. Fifth boundary-watch cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Realized WFP food-assistance cuts plus a formal IPC Phase 5 famine declaration in Borno with documented aid obstruction — August IPC lean-season update is the next checkpoint.
boundaryWatch.note: Nigeria at 18.0 of 100 Critical sits 2.0 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary at 20.0. Record 36.2 million food insecure; 5.5 million without aid. JNIM attacks are non-state. Fourth boundary-proximity watch cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: July natural Bundibugyo Ebola peak window — active. Documented government obstruction of WHO response corridors in Haut-Uele, Ituri, or North Kivu is the floor-crossing trigger. Multi-continent spread (Uganda, France, Germany) does not affect trigger conditions.
boundaryWatch.note: DRC at 2.3 of 100 Critical through cycle 21 of the Ebola emergency. 1,406 cases and 438 deaths; multi-continent spread confirmed (Uganda, France, Germany). July natural peak window active. No approved Bundibugyo vaccine. Contact tracing at approximately 45 percent. Floor-crossing trigger has not occurred.

boundaryWatch.trigger: A documented new mass-repression or lethal crackdown during the Khamenei funeral window (July 4-9) would constitute a new scored harm event. Documented de-escalation or release of detained protesters at scale would be the upgrade trigger. US-Iran Doha talks resume July 11.
boundaryWatch.note: Iran at 2.5 of 100 Critical sits 2.5 points above the floor. Khamenei state funeral July 4-9 now active — Day 1. US-Iran Doha talks paused to July 11. Mojtaba Khamenei leading succession candidate. 784-plus executions year-to-date — a 37-year high. No scored own-conduct today. Fourth floor-proximity watch cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Bengali Muslim and Rohingya expulsions documented in June 2026; detect-delete-deport digital surveillance continuing. Not assessed tonight.
boundaryWatch.note: India at 15.6 of 100 Critical sits 0.6 points above the Critical floor through a twentieth consecutive monitoring cycle. Not assessed tonight.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Formal IPC Phase 5 famine declaration covering Buur Hakaba or Baidoa combined with documented government aid obstruction is the scored change trigger. Famine risk window extends through September 2026.
boundaryWatch.note: Somalia at 4.7 of 100 Critical. Buur Hakaba at IPC AMN Phase 5 with GAM rate 37.1 percent. Not assessed tonight. Eleventh floor-proximity cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Documented discriminatory denial of emergency protection to stateless bidoon or migrant workers during wartime — Equity downgrade trigger. Durable verified expansion of bidoon rights or citizenship recognition — upgrade trigger.
boundaryWatch.note: Kuwait at 25.0 of 100 Developing sits 5.0 points above the Critical boundary. Equity watch flag open on bidoon and migrant-worker emergency-protection performance. Sixth boundary-watch cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Coordinator-level Sahel-band calibration review. Burkina Faso (6.3 of 100) was reassessed in June on comparable UN-documented state-perpetration conduct. Mali's starting-point score of 12.5 is above the likely calibrated range. No timeline set.
boundaryWatch.note: Mali at 12.5 of 100 Critical on a starting-point seed score. The Sahel calibration flag is in its eleventh consecutive day. Burkina Faso dropped from the July 4 priority cluster. Tenth boundary-watch cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: DOJ False Claims Act complaint (kickbacks and disability discrimination) and Barrows v. Humana class action (nH Predict AI claim denials). Either concluded finding confirms the Developing-band designation.
boundaryWatch.note: Humana at 35.2 of 100 Developing, 4.8 points below the Functional boundary. Thirteenth cycle post-downgrade. Not assessed tonight. Neither the DOJ complaint nor the Barrows class action has a fixed hearing date.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Forced cuts that measurably degrade staff or student support are the Accountability watch trigger; capitulation abandoning academic-freedom stance is the Integrity downgrade trigger.
boundaryWatch.note: Princeton University at 57.8 of 100 Functional sits 2.2 points below the Established boundary at 60.0. First formal baseline established July 2. Not assessed tonight. Third boundary-proximity watch cycle.

emergingRisks.risk: Iran's Khamenei funeral window opened July 4 — a six-day political transition period through July 9 during which protest suppression, mass arrest, or significant diplomatic signals are all possible. Iran holds at 2.5 of 100 near-floor Critical. The funeral is procedural; the score turns on new harm. The Assembly of Experts is expected to confirm a successor within the window.
emergingRisks.detail: Iran at 2.5 of 100. 784-plus executions year-to-date. Strait of Hormuz central channel mined. US-Iran Doha talks paused to July 11. Mojtaba Khamenei is the leading succession candidate. A documented lethal crackdown on mourners or demonstrators, or mass arrest of political figures, during July 4-9 constitutes a new scored harm event. Documented de-escalation or release of detained protesters at scale would be the upgrade trigger.

emergingRisks.risk: Russia's July 4 Independence Day strike on Kyiv extends the record July civilian campaign — over 30 killed in three days. The EU Special Tribunal for crimes in Ukraine is active. Russia holds at 0 of 100; Ukraine holds at 50.0 of 100.
emergingRisks.detail: Russia at 0 of 100 — the absolute floor. Ukraine at 50.0 Functional. The 2026 civilian-casualty rate is running above the full-year 2025 rate per the OHCHR July 1 report documenting 62,716 total casualties. The EU Special Tribunal adds a formal accountability mechanism without changing either entity's score. Ukraine's civilian-protection response is the scored variable for Ukraine.

emergingRisks.risk: Sudan's HRW El Obeid imminent-atrocities warning documents 500,000 people at siege risk in the North Kordofan state capital — the RSF's latest offensive front. Combined with Amnesty's El Fasher crimes-against-humanity report from July 3, Sudan's July package is now the highest-severity accumulation in the series.
emergingRisks.detail: Sudan at 0 of 100. Al-Burhan's ceasefire rejection stands. The RSF conduct is non-state; government's own conduct collapse is already priced at the floor. The HRW 'imminent' framing significantly raises the forward probability of a fast-moving humanitarian catastrophe. An implemented civilian-protecting ceasefire with unobstructed aid access is the sole scored upgrade trigger.

emergingRisks.risk: Venezuela's July 8 two-week checkpoint is four days out. The official death toll stands at 2,645 on Day 10 — approximately 50,000 people remain unaccounted. A national emergency fund of $200 million covers under 1 percent of the $37 billion damage estimate.
emergingRisks.detail: Venezuela at 18.0 of 100 Critical, 2.0 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary. The July 8 checkpoint is the standard 14-day mark for distinguishing capacity failure from deliberate obstruction. Action and Accountability watch flags open. Aid acceptance from international partners continues; domestic distribution remains slow and access-restricted.

emergingRisks.risk: Pakistan's cross-border TTP strikes in Afghanistan — June 28-29, 36 civilian deaths disputed — open a new attribution watch. Independent corroboration of deliberate civilian targeting is the scoring threshold. Pakistan enters the priority cluster for the first time at 17.2 of 100.
emergingRisks.detail: Pakistan at 17.2 of 100 Critical, 2.8 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary. The June 28-29 cross-border strikes targeted TTP (non-state) positions on Afghan territory. A 36-civilian death claim came from Afghan Taliban sources, disputed by Pakistan. Single-source, contested, on foreign territory — correctly screened. Monsoon season begins; historical monsoon-flood response performance is a separate scorable dimension. Rotation-state drift corrected from 15.3 composite to published 17.2.

emergingRisks.risk: DRC Ebola's July natural peak window is active. Multi-continent spread confirmed from the prior cycle (Uganda, France, Germany). The floor-crossing trigger — documented government obstruction of WHO response corridors — has not occurred.
emergingRisks.detail: DRC at 2.3 of 100. 1,406 cases and 438 deaths. No approved Bundibugyo vaccine. Contact tracing at approximately 45 percent. A South Sudan Ebola incursion from Haut-Uele province combined with documented failure of South Sudan's own emergency response would constitute a scorable own-conduct event — the first cross-floor epidemiological trigger in the series.

emergingRisks.risk: China's Article 63 diaspora enforcement forward trigger has run four days without a documented action. The Ethnic Unity and Progress Law entered force July 1. The first prosecution, forced rendition, or family detention of a diaspora member remains the scored threshold event for Equity and Boundaries docks from China's current 19.5 of 100.
emergingRisks.detail: China at 19.5 of 100, 0.5 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary. Australia, Canada, the United Kingdom, and the United States are the observable proxies for first Article 63 enforcement. The sub-threshold dock of approximately -1.5 composite points does not cross the change threshold without enforcement documentation.

emergingRisks.risk: EU AI Act becomes fully applicable on August 2 — 29 days away. General-purpose AI transparency obligations, Article 50 labeling, and whistleblower protections activate. xAI and OpenAI have not published compliance roadmaps.
emergingRisks.detail: A post-applicability non-compliance finding constitutes a scored event. Anthropic's compliance infrastructure is documented at 59.1 Functional. OpenAI and xAI compliance gaps will become legally significant on August 2. The EU AI Act is the next sector-wide scored deadline for the AI Labs Index.

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — Iran Political Transition; Khamenei Funeral July 4-9
sectorAlerts.headline: Iran's Khamenei state funeral opens July 4 — the first Supreme Leader transition in 35 years; Mojtaba as leading successor; US-Iran talks paused to July 11; Iran holds at 2.5 of 100.
observations: Iran at 2.5 of 100 near-floor Critical. The July 4-9 Khamenei funeral is the Islamic Republic's first leadership transition since Khomeini's death in 1989. The funeral is procedural — a state ceremony, not a scored harm event. The score turns on new own-conduct, not on institutional succession.

Mojtaba Khamenei is the leading succession candidate. The Assembly of Experts is expected to convene during the July 4-9 window to confirm a successor. Whether the succession process itself generates a scored Governance event — or whether only a new harm is scored — is the methodology clarity question open this week.

US-Iran Doha nuclear talks are paused to July 11 — a specific deadline created by the transition. The paused talks are a diplomatic status, not a scored harm event. Iran's 784-plus executions year-to-date (37-year high), Strait of Hormuz mine deployment, and Gulf strikes remain fully priced into the existing 2.5 of 100 score.

The protest-suppression watch flag is open for the full July 4-9 window. A documented lethal crackdown on mourners or demonstrators, or mass arrest of political figures, during the funeral period would constitute a new scored harm event under the near-floor framework. The July 9 window close and July 11 talks resumption are the next two assessment markers.

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — Russia/Ukraine July 4 Strike; Record July Campaign
sectorAlerts.headline: Russia's July 4 Independence Day strike on Kyiv extends the record July toll; 30-plus killed in three days; EU Special Tribunal active; Ukraine confirmed at 50.0 of 100.
observations: Russia holds at 0 of 100 — the absolute floor — for the thirteenth consecutive cycle. The July 4 Kyiv strike continues a campaign that has killed over 30 civilians in the July 2-4 window. The 2026 civilian-casualty rate is running above the full-year 2025 rate per OHCHR documentation.

Ukraine holds at 50.0 of 100 Functional. Ukraine's civilian-protection response — coordinated metro-sheltering and evacuation — is consistent with the Functional band. Under the benchmark's attribution rule, harm inflicted by one state on another is scored against the source state. The rule has been applied consistently for 13 consecutive cycles.

The EU Special Tribunal for crimes in Ukraine is active. This adds a formal accountability mechanism. Russia's scoring is anchored to documented own-conduct, not tribunal outcomes. 20,500-plus Ukrainian children have been documented as forcibly deported.

Three rotation-state rank drifts were corrected this cycle: China rank (154 to 148), Ukraine rank (48 to 53), and Russia rank (180 to 188) — all corrected to published index values. These are rank-only corrections from pre-existing drift, not proposal artifacts.

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — Sudan and South Sudan: HRW Imminent-Atrocities Warning; Dual Floor Entities
sectorAlerts.headline: HRW's El Obeid imminent-atrocities warning documents 500,000 people at siege risk; Amnesty El Fasher CAH report stands; Sudan holds at 0 of 100; South Sudan's Phase 5 famine risk reinforces floor.
observations: Sudan (0 of 100): Human Rights Watch's July 3 El Obeid warning adds a third major evidence item to Sudan's July package — 500,000 people at siege risk in the North Kordofan state capital plus 105,000 IDPs. The Amnesty International El Fasher crimes-against-humanity report from July 3 stands. Both items are RSF (non-state) conduct. Sudan's government collapse is priced at the floor.

The HRW 'imminent' framing is analytically significant. An imminent-atrocities warning from a Tier-3 source means the window for civilian protection is narrow. The scored upgrade trigger — Al-Burhan implementing a civilian-protecting ceasefire with unobstructed aid access — remains the only direction of travel. No movement toward the trigger has been documented.

South Sudan (0 of 100): 7.8 million food insecure; 73,300 at Phase 5 Catastrophe — a 160 percent increase since June; famine risk confirmed in four counties. This is a floor-reinforcement event. No positive countervailing state conduct was identified. The cross-floor Ebola incursion risk from DRC's Haut-Uele province remains an open forward trigger for South Sudan.

The structural observation: the two most severe evidence packages this cycle (Sudan HRW/Amnesty, South Sudan Phase 5) are for floor entities that cannot move lower. The upgrade trigger is the only lever. Monitoring continues.

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — Pakistan New Priority Entrant; El Salvador Day 3 Carry-Forward; China Article 63 Day 4
sectorAlerts.headline: Pakistan enters the priority cluster for the first time at 17.2 of 100; El Salvador's band-crossing proposal carries forward a third day at 15.0 proposed; China's Article 63 law is in day 4 without a documented enforcement.
observations: Pakistan (17.2 of 100): first priority-cluster appearance. The June 28-29 cross-border strikes on TTP positions in Afghanistan generated a 36-civilian-death claim from Afghan Taliban sources, disputed by Pakistan. The harm was inflicted on Afghan territory (Afghanistan's protection duty). Correctly screened as disputed-attribution. Pakistan's rotation-state composite had drifted to 15.3; corrected to 17.2 (published). The 2.0-point correction is the largest single-entity composite correction in the July series.

El Salvador (published 20.3 of 100): the July 2 proposal (proposed 15.0 of 100, Critical band) carries forward as pending on July 4. Third carry-forward cycle. No new evidence identified. Duplicate-control rule correctly applied. Published score remains 20.3 of 100. The Cristosal exile, indefinite re-election amendment, 86 political prisoners, and child life-sentences remain the proposal's evidence basis.

China (19.5 of 100): the Ethnic Unity Law's Article 63 diaspora liability has been in force for four days without a documented enforcement action (prosecution, forced rendition, or family detention of a diaspora member). The sub-threshold -1.5 composite dock stands as a forward trigger. Australia, Canada, the United Kingdom, and the United States are the observable proxies for first enforcement.

Venezuela (18.0 of 100): Day 10 earthquake response. Official toll 2,645 — up 50 from the Day 9 count. Approximately 50,000 unaccounted. The July 8 checkpoint is four days out. This is the fifth consecutive boundary-watch cycle at 2.0 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary.

sectorAlerts.sector: Methodology — 15 False-Positive Screens Tie Record; Four Rotation-State Corrections
sectorAlerts.headline: Fifteen false-positive screens tie the series record for a second straight cycle; four rotation-state integrity corrections applied — the largest single-cycle correction batch in the July series.
observations: The 15 false-positive screens match the prior series record (set July 3). The breakdown: attribution/other-state (Ukraine, Lebanon, DRC M23); cross-border disputed attribution (Pakistan — new category for July 4); non-state (Sudan RSF, Yemen Houthi, Mali JNIM, Nigeria JNIM/ISWAP); exogenous (Venezuela quake, DRC Ebola); pre-adjudication (UnitedHealth); double-count (China, Nigeria); duplicate-control (El Salvador); floor-limitation (Russia, Sudan, South Sudan, Yemen).

All seven false-positive categories were active simultaneously for the second consecutive cycle — the first time this has happened in back-to-back cycles. All 15 screens resolved to confirmation without exception. No score was incorrectly moved. Framework stability under maximum simultaneous pressure is validated.

Four rotation-state integrity corrections: China rank (154 to 148), Ukraine rank (48 to 53), Russia rank (180 to 188), and Pakistan composite (15.3 to 17.2) and rank (147 to 154). These are pre-existing drift corrections to published values — not proposal artifacts. The Pakistan composite correction (2.0 points) is the largest single-entity composite correction in the July series.

The Sahel calibration flag (Mali vs. Burkina Faso) is in its eleventh consecutive day without a coordinator review timeline. Burkina Faso dropped from the July 4 priority cluster. The gap persists at the state-conduct level; JNIM non-state harm does not resolve it.

methodologyNotes.name: False-Positive Attribution Framework — 15 Screens (Series Record Tied)
methodologyNotes.description: Fifteen false-positive screens were applied this cycle — tying the series record set July 3. The seven active categories: (1) attribution/other-state (Ukraine: July 4 Kyiv strike scored against Russia; Lebanon: IDF strikes scored against Israel; DRC M23: Eastern DRC harm scored against Rwanda-backed forces); (2) cross-border disputed attribution — NEW category for July series (Pakistan: June 28-29 TTP strikes on Afghan territory; 36 civilian deaths from single-source Taliban claim, disputed by Pakistan); (3) non-state (Sudan: RSF El Obeid and El Fasher; Yemen: Houthi UN detentions; Mali: JNIM Bamako siege; Nigeria: JNIM/ISWAP attacks); (4) exogenous (Venezuela: earthquake; DRC: Ebola disease); (5) double-count (China: Article 63 already-priced; Nigeria: Phase 5 already-priced June 19); (6) pre-adjudication (UnitedHealth: DOJ probes not adjudicated); (7) floor-limitation (Russia, Sudan, South Sudan, Yemen: all at 0 of 100 floor; duplicate-control for El Salvador). All 15 correctly disposed of under the existing framework.

methodologyNotes.name: Rotation-State Integrity Corrections — Four This Cycle (Largest Batch in July Series)
methodologyNotes.description: Four pre-existing rotation-state drifts corrected to published index values: (1) China rank drifted from 154 to published 148; (2) Ukraine rank drifted from 48 to published 53; (3) Russia rank drifted from 180 to published 188; (4) Pakistan composite drifted from 15.3 to published 17.2, and rank from 147 to published 154. The Pakistan composite correction of 2.0 points is the largest single-entity composite correction in the July series. All corrections reflect the core principle: rotation-state must track the published index, not a proposed score or an intermediate entry error. No proposed or unapplied values were written to rotation-state. The anti-misread protocol correctly identified all four drifts before they could affect assessment baselines.

methodologyNotes.name: Duplicate-Control Rule (El Salvador) — Third Cycle, No Second Proposal
methodologyNotes.description: The July 4 El Salvador assessment encountered the same evidence basis (indefinite re-election amendment, Cristosal exile, 86 political prisoners, child life-sentences) already captured in the July 2 pending downgrade proposal. The duplicate-control rule was applied: when a pending proposal covers the same evidence basis and no materially new evidence has emerged, no second proposal is emitted. The pending proposal stands as the operative change record. The baseline for the July 4 assessment is the published value (20.3 of 100), not the proposed value (15.0 of 100). This is the third consecutive cycle (July 2, 3, 4) applying the same duplicate-control disposition to El Salvador.

forwardTriggers.trigger: 14-day reassessment checkpoint — independently corroborated suppression or falsification of casualty data, or documented deliberate aid obstruction, is the downgrade trigger from 18.0 of 100. Sustained effective transparent relief is the upgrade trigger. Official toll 2,645; Day 10.

Close of Khamenei state funeral window — documented lethal crackdown on mourners or demonstrators, or mass arrest of political figures, during July 4-9 constitutes a new scored harm event from the near-floor 2.5 of 100. Funeral without lethal suppression continues the already-priced pattern.

US-Iran Doha nuclear talks scheduled to resume July 11. A resumed negotiation is a diplomatic status event, not a scored harm. A formal agreement with verifiable compliance would constitute scored positive own-conduct; a breakdown combined with new provocation (strait closure, nuclear escalation) would be a scored harm event.
Full resolution-phase reassessment due. Conversion trigger: reversal of the June 8 codified-impunity law and release of detained union leaders.

Q2 2026 earnings call — material DOJ disclosures, investor-facing admissions about probe scope, indictment, or settlement announcements are scored triggers under Accountability and Integrity dimensions.

July natural Bundibugyo Ebola peak window active. 1,406 cases and 438 deaths. Multi-continent spread confirmed (Uganda, France, Germany). Documented government obstruction of WHO response corridors is the floor-crossing trigger toward 0 of 100.
EU AI Act full applicability — general-purpose AI transparency obligations activate. No public compliance roadmap from xAI.
EU AI Act full applicability — same obligations apply. 42-state attorney general proceedings ongoing.
EU AI Act full applicability — 29 days away. Anthropic's compliance infrastructure is documented. White House 30-day vetting framework resolution is the next forward factor.
August IPC lean-season update — IPC Phase 5 Borno trajectory and actual WFP food-assistance cuts are scored triggers from the June 19 band crossing to 18.0 of 100.

Famine declaration window open through September 2026. Buur Hakaba and Baidoa at IPC AMN Phase 5. A formal IPC Phase 5 famine declaration combined with documented government aid obstruction is a scored change trigger.

2027 election under the July 2025 indefinite re-election constitutional amendment. Enforcement of the amendment in the 2027 election is the next structured forward trigger. A further human-rights-defender imprisonment or mass-detention escalation constitutes a deeper downgrade trigger from the proposed Critical band. Due-process restoration and civil-society reinstatement are the upgrade triggers.

Independently verified deliberate civilian targeting in the June 28-29 cross-border strikes, or a new documented mass-repression event, is the downgrade trigger from 17.2 of 100. Ceasefire adherence with civilian protection and durable Afghan-deportation reversal are the upgrade triggers. Monsoon-flood response is a second independent scored dimension beginning this month.

First documented Article 63 enforcement action under the Ethnic Unity and Progress Law — prosecution, forced rendition, or family detention of a diaspora member. Law entered force July 1; four days without enforcement as of July 4.

Russia at 0 of 100 — the absolute floor. Cessation of deliberate attacks on civilians, return of forcibly deported children, and cooperation with the EU Special Tribunal are the upgrade-trigger conditions.

An implemented civilian-protecting ceasefire with unobstructed humanitarian access is the scored positive own-conduct trigger. El Fasher is the sentinel geography for the next major observable event; HRW El Obeid imminent-atrocities warning makes North Kordofan an equally critical monitoring zone.
A new ceasefire AND Lebanese Armed Forces assuming sovereign control of southern Lebanon AND reconstruction delivery are the scored positive-conduct triggers from 17.2 of 100.

A realized Ebola incursion from DRC's Haut-Uele province combined with documented failure of South Sudan's own emergency response would constitute a scorable own-conduct event — the first cross-floor epidemiological trigger in the series.

Coordinator-level Sahel-band calibration review — Mali (12.5 of 100) versus Burkina Faso (6.3 of 100). Eleventh consecutive day without a timeline. Burkina Faso dropped from the July 4 priority cluster.

dailyOpeningQuestion.text: Iran holds at 2.5 of 100 as Khamenei's funeral opens July 4. If the Assembly of Experts confirms Mojtaba as Supreme Leader this week, does the succession itself constitute new scored conduct on Governance — or does the score hold until a distinct harm event occurs under the new leadership?
dailyOpeningQuestion.tensionFraming: Iran's 2.5 of 100 is anchored to documented own-conduct. Political succession inherits the existing repression architecture but generates no new harm by itself.
